titleStmt.title: Letter from T West to Lady Clonbrock, 23 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: T West

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from a prisoner of war, T West, Connaught Rangers, to Lady Clonbrock (1839-1928). West was incarcerated in Limburg, Germany at the time of this letter. West writes to thank Lady Clonbrock for the Christmas parcel she sent him and asks for a pair of boots.
Augusta Caroline Dillon (née Crofton) was the wife of Luke Gerald Dillon (1834-1917), the 4th Baron of Clonbrock, Co. Galway and the daughter of Lord Crofton of Mote Park (Edward Henry Churchill Crofton, 3rd Baron), Co. Roscommon. Aged 75 at the outbreak of war, Lady Clonbrock, worked closely with the Irish Women's Association to send basic necessities to Irish POWs. Many of her care packages went to members of the Connaught Rangers imprisoned in Limburg near Cologne.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Kriegsgefangenenlager.
Limburg , 23rd November 1915 Dear Lady Clonbrock, Just a card thank
—ing you for your Very Kind Christmas
parcel. But I am going to intrude on your
very great generosity again & I hope you
will not think me too forward. I am in need
of a pair of Boots size 6 if you could
manage to send me a pair I would be very thank
full. A pair with plenty of nails trusting you will
be so good & oblige. Yours sincerely, T.West CRs
Gefangenenlager
Limburg (Lahn).
This is my address:
Kriegsgefangenen—Sendung
Mr. 7258 T. West Conn. Rangers
II. Bat. 4 Comp. No.
Limburg (Lahn) Germany
(Answer on Post-Card or by open letter, please).
Feldpostbrief
Kriegsgefangenen—Sendung
Address:
To Lady Clonbrock Clonbrock,
Ahascragh,
Co.Galway
Ireland.
